consistency.with_dnd_norms: Consistent. This feature represents the choice of a subclass (Monastic Tradition) that occurs at level 3 for the Monk class.
consistency.internal_consistency: Consistent. It correctly links the level prerequisite (3rd) with the action (committing to a tradition) and lists the future levels where more features from this choice will be gained (6th, 11th, 17th).

quality.clarity: High. The description is clear about when the choice is made and what its effect is.
quality.completeness: Sufficient for an overview. It establishes the framework for tradition-based progression.
quality.formatting: Adequate. Includes a cross-reference link (`[monastic tradition](#section-monastic-traditions)`), which is helpful for navigation.

effects: Commits the character to a specific Monastic Tradition.
Establishes future feature progression points tied to the chosen tradition (levels 3, 6, 11, 17).
mechanics.dependencies: Relies on external definitions for the specific traditions themselves.

analysis.suggestions: No significant changes needed. This is a standard structure for defining a class-defining choice feature.
